data_architecture.data_ownership_rule: Each domain module owns writes to its core entities; other modules reference by stable IDs or read models.
data_architecture.history_strategy: Use audit events and explicit snapshots for imports, price calculations, quote outputs, and approval transitions.
data_architecture.tenant_model: Workspace-scoped multi-tenant data with every business entity carrying workspace ownership.

relationships: ImportBatch belongs to SupplierSource and produces Product or ServiceOffering candidates
Product and ServiceOffering belong to Workspace and optionally Supplier
Bundle and ProductionRecipe reference Products, ServiceOfferings, CostComponents, and PriceCalculations
Quote references Customer, QuoteLines, PriceSnapshots, Bundles, Recipes, Products, and Services
GeneratedOutput references Quote or future Catalog/PriceBook/Proposal records

data_risks.risk: Customer-specific pricing and margin data may leak across workspaces or customer contexts.
data_risks.mitigation: Enforce workspace scope at query layer and authorization layer; snapshot customer-specific pricing in generated outputs.

data_risks.risk: Supplier import records may overwrite curated normalized data.
data_risks.mitigation: Use import staging and human commit workflow; preserve source provenance.

architecture_decisions.decision: Use immutable pricing snapshots for generated outputs.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Quotes and price books must remain auditable even when supplier costs or pricing rules change later.

architecture_decisions.decision: Use import staging before normalized asset writes.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Supplier files and service tables are inconsistent and require review.

stage_4_handoff_notes: UX must show import staging versus committed asset records clearly.
Pricing snapshot and margin status should be visible in quote review.
stage_5_handoff_notes: Enforce workspace scoping in every repository/query abstraction.
Add audit events for pricing, import commit, quote approval, and generated output sharing.
